children.heading: Reservation of funds
children.label: a Reservation of funds
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this part, of amounts made available to the Attorney General to carry out programs relating to offender incarceration, the Attorney General shall reserve $35,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2011 through 2015 to carry out this section.

children.label: i
children.text: construction and maintenance of jails on Indian land for the incarceration of offenders subject to tribal jurisdiction;

children.label: ii
children.text: entering into contracts with private entities to increase the efficiency of the construction of tribal jails; and

children.label: iii
children.text: developing and implementing alternatives to incarceration in tribal jails;

children.label: B
children.text: to Indian tribes for the construction of tribal justice centers that combine tribal police, courts, and corrections services to address violations of tribal civil and criminal laws;

children.label: C
children.text: to consortia of Indian tribes for purposes of constructing and operating regional detention centers on Indian land for long-term incarceration of offenders subject to tribal jurisdiction, as the applicable consortium determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Federal share
children.label: 3 Federal share
children.text: Because of the Federal nature and responsibility for providing public safety on Indian land, the Federal share of the cost of any activity carried out using a grant under this subsection shall be 100 percent.

children.heading: Applications
children.label: c Applications
children.text: To be eligible to receive a grant under this section, an Indian tribe or consortium of Indian tribes, as applicable, shall submit to the Attorney General an application in such form and containing such information as the Attorney General may by regulation require.

children.label: A
children.text: construction, operation, and maintenance of juvenile (in accordance with ) and adult detention facilities (including regional facilities) in Indian country;section 2453(a)(3) of title 25

children.label: B
children.text: contracting with State and local detention centers, on approval of the affected tribal governments; and

children.label: C
children.text: alternatives to incarceration, developed in cooperation with tribal court systems;
an assessment and consideration of the construction of Federal detention facilities in Indian country; and
any other alternatives as the Attorney General, in coordination with the Bureau of Indian Affairs and in consultation with Indian tribes, determines to be necessary.
